title: Fix vec3.js shim fractional offset destruction in immutable operations
description:
**Source:** External audit (Bug 1) | **Council:** P0 | **Option:** B

**Problem:** `vec3.js` floors x/y/z at construction and all arithmetic methods (offset, plus, minus, multiply, unit, normalize, etc.) route through `toVec3()` which re-floors. Since `.floored()` returns `this` (not a new object), prismarine-world's `block.position = pos.floored()` means `block.position` IS our broken shim. Mineflayer's internal digging/placing code calls `block.position.offset(0.5, 0.5, 0.5)` which floors back to integers — bot aims at block corners instead of centers.

**Blast radius:** 100% of dig and place calls pass through toVec3. This is the hidden root cause behind Sprint 41/52 placement workarounds (facing-aware retries, 6-face fallback, scaffold logic).

**Fix (Option B from council):** Stop flooring in immutable arithmetic ops. Make `.floored()` return a NEW shim object with floored values instead of `this`. This matches the real prismarine-vector Vec3 contract.

**Key changes in vec3.js:**
1. Store raw (unfloored) values in `self.x/y/z`
2. `.floored()` returns `toVec3(Math.floor(x), Math.floor(y), Math.floor(z))` — a NEW object
3. Arithmetic methods (offset, plus, minus, etc.) return `toVec3(x+dx, y+dy, z+dz)` without intermediate flooring (they already add to floored values, but the result gets re-floored — stop that)

**Note:** The existing `index.js` call sites all pass integer coordinates to `toVec3()`, so this change is safe for direct calls. The impact is on objects returned by `bot.blockAt()` that flow into Mineflayer's own code.

**Files:** `MineflayerAdapter/vec3.js`

**Validation:** After fix, verify `block.position.offset(0.5, 0.5, 0.5)` returns fractional values. Run E2E placement test to confirm aim centers on block faces. Do NOT remove Sprint 41/52 workaround code yet — validate first.
